mission.title: fluentd: Fluentd in_tail "unreadable" file causes "following tail of <file>" to stop and no logs pushed
mission.description: Fluentd in_tail "unreadable" file causes "following tail of <file>" to stop and no logs pushed. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify fluentd troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your fluentd deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n fluentd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluentd
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluentd -n fluentd --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review fluentd configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant fluentd configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n fluentd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluentd
kubectl get configmap -n fluentd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=fluentd
```
### Describe the bug

After a warning of an "unreadable" (likely due to rotation), no more logs were pushed (in_tail + pos_file).
Reloading config or restarting fluentd sorts the issue.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Fluentd in_tail "unreadable" file causes "following tail of…
steps.description:
In #3614 some users reported that in_tail rarely stops tailing. It seems
it's caused when skipping update_watcher due to unexpected duplicate
pos_file entries. The root cause is unknown yet. To investigate it, show
more infomation when it happens. Ideally it should't happen so that log
level "warn"
```yaml
If you see such log message, please report it.
Hello, @ashie! We have something similar to: "no more log pushed" and found duplication in `pos` file. 
Like this:
```

steps.title: Confirm Fluentd in_tail "unreadable" file causes… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluentd -n fluentd --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n fluentd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
In #3614 some users reported that in_tail rarely stops tailing. It seems
it's caused when skipping update_watcher due to unexpected duplicate
pos_file entries. The root cause is unknown yet. To investigate it, show
more infomation when it happens. Ideally it should't happen so that log
level "warn" is desired for it (I can't reproduce this issue yet).
Same with the title
codeSnippets:
If you see such log message, please report it.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with fluentd installed or the issue environment reproducible.
